questions.question: The term ___ radiant temperature describes the area-weighted average temperature of all surfaces within a room.

Water has many characteristics that make it ideal for a hydronic system. It is readily available, nontoxic, nonflammable, and has one of the highest ____ storage abilities of any material known to man.
Thermal radiation is probably the most understood mode of heat transfer.
Heat always moves from an area of ____ temperature to an area of ____ temperature.
Maintaining comfort is simply a matter of supplying heat to the body.
Ideally, the water in a hydronic heating system remains in the system year after year.
When fluid motion is caused by either a circulator, blower, or fan, the resulting heat transfer is more specifically called ____.
Heat is our perception of atomic vibrations within a material. Our means of expressing the intensity of these vibrations is called ___.
All heating systems consist of devices that convert one form of energy into another.
The emergence of electrically powered ____ made it possible to move water at higher flow rates through much more elaborate piping systems.
Buildings with hydronic heating systems have shown ____ heating energy use compared to equivalent structures with forced-air heating systems.
Properly designed hydronic systems control both the air temperature and surface temperatures of rooms to maintain optimal system water level.
Modern controls can maintain room air temperature +/- ___ °F of the desired setpoint temperature.
Providing comfort is the primary objective of any heating system designer or installer.
Hydronic heating systems use water (or water-based solutions) to move thermal energy from where it is produced to where it is needed.
In single-loop systems, the ____ prevents hot water in the boiler from slowly circulating through the distribution system when the circulator is off.
The simplest hydronic system can be described as a loop or piping circuit.
An air separator is designed to separate air from water and eject it from the system. The process is ____ the water is heated.
In single-loop systems, the flow-check valve allows hot water in the boiler to slowly circulate through the distribution system when the circulator is off.
The difference in energy use by various heating and cooling distribution systems can be quantified using the concept of ____.
A properly designed and installed hydronic system can operate with virtually no detectable sound levels in the occupied areas of a home.
Distribution efficiency (Btu/h/watt) is equal to the rate of thermal energy delivery divided by the electrical power required to operate the distribution system.
An air separator is designed to separate air from water and eject it from the system. The process is ____ the water is heated.
Hydronic systems that transfer the majority of their heat by thermal radiation reduce air temperature ____ and thus reduce heat loss through ceilings.
Heat emitters that use fans or blowers to force air through a heat exchanger are usually called fan coils or air ____.
